subject: [PATCH v4 3/5] locking: Add contended_release tracepoint to sleepable locks

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary: Reviewer Usama Arif pointed out a compilation error due to the use of 'waiter' variable outside its scope in the slowpath unlock path of sleepable locks, specifically in mutex.c.
reviews.raw_body:
This won't compile? waiter is declared in the if block, so you are using
it outside scope here.

reviews.author: Dmitry Ilvokhin (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about the scope of the waiter variable in the slowpath unlock paths, explaining that it is declared and assigned before the if block where the tracepoint is called.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for the feedback, Usama.

waiter is declared at function scope, right on top. It's also assigned
before the if block, so it's still in scope at the tracepoint.

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that changes made on top of mm-new branch may have altered waiter declaration, potentially affecting contended_release tracepoint functionality.
reviews.raw_body:
Ah ok, I was reviewing on top of mm-new branch from today where waiter
is declared in the if block. Probably something changed related to
locking/tracing and its not in mm-new yet.

2026-03-31.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release to the Linux kernel's locking subsystem. The contended_release tracepoint fires on the holder side when a contended lock is released, allowing for correlation of lock hold time under contention with waiter events by lock address. The patch modifies the slowpath unlock paths of various sleepable locks (mutex, rtmutex, semaphore, rwsem, percpu-rwsem, and RT-specific rwbase locks) to fire the tracepoint before releasing the lock or waking the waiter. This enables more detailed tracing and debugging of locking behavior in the kernel.

2026-04-07.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release to the Linux kernel's locking subsystem. The contended_release tracepoint fires on the holder side when a contended lock is released, allowing for correlation of lock hold time under contention with waiter events by lock address. The patch modifies the slowpath unlock paths of various sleepable locks (mutex, rtmutex, semaphore, rwsem, percpu-rwsem, and RT-specific rwbase locks) to fire the tracepoint before releasing the lock or waking the waiter. This enables more detailed tracing and debugging of locking behavior in the kernel.
